required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The wording must explicitly separate “release tag already exists” from “default branch still lags behind”.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The wording must preserve the bounded Telegram Desktop baseline and the explicit no-go for calls.

scenarios.scenario-1: The branch sync gate proves a clean tree and a safe fast-forward from origin/master to the approved baseline.
scenarios.scenario-2: Dirty tree, baseline mismatch, or non-bounded branch drift blocks the master push.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The gate must reject any state where `HEAD != v0.3.2` even if the tree is otherwise clean.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The gate must distinguish controller-owned planning edits in progress from the final clean-tree condition required before pushing master.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: The gate must preserve a bounded ahead-set summary so another agent can audit what is being published to origin/master.

scenarios.scenario-1: origin/master is updated to the same commit as the approved stable local baseline.
scenarios.scenario-2: The push fails or origin/master still does not match the approved baseline after the sync attempt.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The push step must verify post-push equality `origin/master == HEAD` rather than assuming success from push output alone.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The push step must preserve that the visible branch baseline equals the already published v0.3.2 commit.

scenarios.scenario-1: The released commit behind v0.3.2 is frozen as the only allowed target for origin/master.
scenarios.scenario-2: The corrective master sync would still derive its publish target from the newer local HEAD.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The target-freeze step must preserve the exact v0.3.2 commit identity as the allowed publish target.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The step must explicitly record that local HEAD is newer and is therefore excluded from the corrective publication step.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'v0.3.2|master|released snapshot|released commit|local HEAD' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md

scenarios.scenario-1: Docs explain that the corrective master sync publishes the released v0.3.2 snapshot only.
scenarios.scenario-2: Docs still imply the sync publishes the current local branch tip or a wider scope than the released baseline.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The wording must explicitly separate the released commit from the newer local planning state.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The wording must preserve that the corrective sync exists only to align GitHub default-branch browsing to the already released baseline.

scenarios.scenario-1: The corrective gate proves the tree is clean and that pushing v0.3.2:master will not publish newer local commits.
scenarios.scenario-2: Dirty tree, unresolved released target, or accidental dependence on HEAD blocks the corrective master sync.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The gate must reject any attempt to substitute `git push origin master` for the bounded `git push origin v0.3.2:master` publish path.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The gate must preserve a bounded summary of commits in `v0.3.2..HEAD` as excluded local-only planning state.

scenarios.scenario-1: origin/master is updated exactly to the released v0.3.2 commit.
scenarios.scenario-2: The push fails or origin/master still does not equal v0.3.2 after the corrective sync attempt.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The corrective push step must use the explicit refspec `v0.3.2:master`.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Post-push verification must compare `origin/master` directly to `v0.3.2`, not to local HEAD.

scenarios.scenario-1: The shared datagram contract preserves association identity, target address, and bounded payload semantics.
scenarios.scenario-2: Datagram envelopes drift into transport-specific or unbounded shapes that later agents cannot reason about safely.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The datagram contract must stay transport-agnostic and must not hardcode WSS frame details.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Association identity, target address, and size bounds must remain explicit and reviewable.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: The contract must explicitly distinguish relay-client address, remote target address, payload bytes, and association ownership.

scenarios.scenario-1: SOCKS5 UDP ASSOCIATE returns a governed relay bind and normalizes relay packets into datagram envelopes.
scenarios.scenario-2: Malformed UDP relay packets, unsupported fragmentation, or foreign-source packets are rejected deterministically.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The existing CONNECT path must remain intact while UDP ASSOCIATE adds a separate governed relay surface.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Relay-bind and packet-source validation must be deterministic and bounded.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: Unsupported SOCKS5 UDP fragmentation must be rejected deterministically rather than partially relayed.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-4: The relay socket must not accept datagrams from a foreign client source after the association is established.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-5: The live SOCKS5 listener must negotiate UDP ASSOCIATE through its per-connection runtime path instead of rejecting command 0x03 before the governed control session starts.

scenarios.scenario-1: Active UDP associations, relay metadata, and idle cleanup stay deterministic.
scenarios.scenario-2: Association state leaks, wrong association lookup, or idle timeout cleanup becomes ambiguous.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Association lifecycle must remain explicit, bounded, and independently reviewable from TCP session state.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Idle timeout cleanup and explicit close must both release relay ownership without leaving reusable stale association state.

scenarios.scenario-1: Datagram association open, outbound dispatch, inbound dispatch, and cleanup follow the contract.
scenarios.scenario-2: Datagram ownership, association routing, or cleanup order drifts from the governed lifecycle.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Outbound and inbound datagram handling must remain associated with the correct UDP session identity.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The manager must preserve a clean separation between association-open, outbound-dispatch, inbound-dispatch, and cleanup trajectories.

scenarios.scenario-1: Governed datagrams are encapsulated in WSS frames and recovered without changing the stream path semantics.
scenarios.scenario-2: Frame encoding, decoding, or auth-bound routing leaks datagram context across associations.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The WSS datagram path must remain distinct from the existing stream path while preserving the same auth and TLS boundary.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Datagram frame encoding must preserve association identity and target metadata without leaking packets across tunnels.

scenarios.scenario-1: The server-side relay sends outbound UDP datagrams and returns inbound packets to the correct association.
scenarios.scenario-2: Wrong target mapping, inbound-source ambiguity, or relay leaks break bounded call-path reasoning.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Outbound and inbound UDP relay semantics must remain association-scoped and deterministic.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Inbound relay must classify foreign or unexpected source packets deterministically instead of returning them to the client association.

scenarios.scenario-1: The governed datagram transport path is selected deterministically for the initial WSS-backed UDP phase.
scenarios.scenario-2: Datagram transport selection becomes ambiguous, parallel, or silently wider than the approved initial scope.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The initial datagram transport selector must remain explicitly bounded to the approved phase scope and future extension points.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The selector must make it obvious in logs and failure packets that the initial datagram phase uses the WSS-backed path and does not yet prove iroh datagram parity.

scenarios.scenario-1: Docs describe Telegram Desktop calls as a bounded UDP-capable extension to the existing governed path.
scenarios.scenario-2: Docs overclaim universal unblock or imply that calls support is proven before the dedicated calls wave runs.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Public claims must stay bounded to the tested Telegram Desktop calls setup and must not claim a new Telegram-specific protocol.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Docs must explicitly separate the already green text/file TCP envelope from the new UDP media envelope under validation.

scenarios.scenario-1: A dedicated Telegram calls wave proves signaling green, UDP media-path green, and reconnect classification under the governed setup.
scenarios.scenario-2: Call setup reuses the old TCP-only evidence model and cannot distinguish signaling success from media-path failure.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The calls wave must separate signaling success from UDP media success.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Failure packets must preserve the first divergent layer rather than collapsing back into generic Telegram smoke evidence.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: Reconnect evidence for calls must show whether a fresh UDP association was created or a stale one was incorrectly reused.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'call-failure packet|media-flow packet|call-setup packet|reconnect packet|LV-009|UDP' docs/verification-plan.xml docs/OPERATORS.md

scenarios.scenario-1: Call setup, media flow, reconnect, and failure packets stay separable and reusable by later agents.
scenarios.scenario-2: Call-path evidence is dumped as one opaque packet that hides the first divergent block.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Calls evidence must remain packetized into setup, media, reconnect, and failure classes.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The media packet must not be inferred from signaling-only evidence or from a green text-message path.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: The reconnect packet must carry fresh association identity evidence instead of only restating the first call logs.

module-checks.check-1: ssh root@$N0WSS_SERVER_HOST 'uname -a && id && command -v bash && command -v systemctl && command -v ss'
module-checks.check-2: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'uname -a && id && command -v bash && command -v systemctl && command -v ss'

scenarios.scenario-1: Both live hosts yield baseline identity and tooling evidence before the calls deploy starts.
scenarios.scenario-2: The calls wave does not proceed if either host lacks SSH reachability or baseline tooling.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Host baseline evidence must be captured before deploy output, service state, or Telegram call symptoms are interpreted.

scenarios.scenario-1: The approved UDP-capable build is deployed to both hosts using the governed layout.
scenarios.scenario-2: Deploy drift or missing staged binaries blocks the live calls wave before readiness is interpreted.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The live calls deploy path must remain the governed deploy path and must not fall back to ad hoc host mutation.

scenarios.scenario-1: Readiness evidence includes service state, bound listener state, and UDP-capable runtime markers before app testing starts.
scenarios.scenario-2: Operator handoff is blocked if readiness lacks either service-manager proof or the expected runtime anchors.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Readiness must prove both the already-known signaling path and the new UDP-capable runtime markers before any operator call attempt is trusted.

scenarios.scenario-1: The handoff packet gives one exact local-workstation Telegram Desktop route for the calls wave.
scenarios.scenario-2: No live calls result is trusted if the SSH-forward or SOCKS5 handoff is still ambiguous.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The handoff must tell the operator to keep SSH-forward liveness evidence separate from UDP media diagnosis.

scenarios.scenario-1: One operator voice call produces signaling evidence plus UDP association and media-path evidence.
scenarios.scenario-2: Voice-call failure is classified at the first missing UDP layer instead of being reduced to a generic Telegram symptom.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: A voice-call packet must keep signaling evidence separate from UDP media evidence.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Voice success is not inferred from ringing or answered-call UI without the UDP markers.

scenarios.scenario-1: One operator video call produces its own setup and media evidence packet.
scenarios.scenario-2: Video-call failure remains separate from the voice result so a narrower media defect can be isolated.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Video-call evidence must remain separate from the voice-call packet rather than inheriting the voice result.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Video success is not inferred from a green voice call without its own media markers.

scenarios.scenario-1: A second call attempt yields evidence for a new UDP association after the first call ends.
scenarios.scenario-2: Reconnect evidence rejects stale association reuse and points at the lifecycle layer if it occurs.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The reconnect packet must show a fresh association lifecycle after the prior call ends or idles out.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Reused stale relay state is a failure, not a reconnect success.

scenarios.scenario-1: The calls wave stores separate packets for call setup, media flow, reconnect, and failure classification.
scenarios.scenario-2: A blended transcript without the separate packet boundaries is treated as insufficient evidence.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The packet set must remain split into voice, video, reconnect, and failure evidence instead of one blended manual summary.

scenarios.scenario-1: The final decision is anchored to the tested Telegram Desktop setup only.
scenarios.scenario-2: The live calls phase rejects any broader claim than the tested setup and captured evidence support.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The final decision must separately state the bounded result for voice, video, and reconnect behavior if they diverge.

scenarios.scenario-1: The repaired baseline containing commit 5d8b598 is redeployed to both live hosts through the governed deploy path.
scenarios.scenario-2: The rerun wave stops if the repaired baseline is not what reaches the live hosts.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The rerun deploy packet must distinguish the repaired baseline from the older pre-fix Phase-19 deploy state.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The rerun deploy wording must stay bounded to the ingress repair and must not imply that working Telegram calls are already proven.

scenarios.scenario-1: Post-fix readiness proves the live listener no longer rejects UDP ASSOCIATE before the governed control session starts.
scenarios.scenario-2: If UDP ASSOCIATE still never appears, the rerun stops at readiness rather than reusing the old call packet blindly.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The post-fix readiness packet must explicitly prove whether the first divergent layer moved beyond UDP ingress.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The readiness packet must keep generic TCP signaling readiness separate from repaired UDP ingress proof.

scenarios.scenario-1: The repaired voice rerun yields a new bounded packet that either reaches UDP ingress or classifies a deeper first divergent block.
scenarios.scenario-2: The repaired voice rerun must not silently reuse the original Phase-19 no-go packet.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The repaired voice rerun must classify the first missing post-fix media marker instead of collapsing back to a generic key-exchange symptom.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: If BLOCK_HANDLE_UDP_ASSOCIATE appears, the packet must promote the next missing datagram marker to the first divergent block.

scenarios.scenario-1: The repaired video rerun yields its own bounded packet after the ingress fix.
scenarios.scenario-2: The repaired video rerun cannot inherit the repaired voice result without its own packetized evidence.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The repaired video rerun must remain separately classified even if the repaired voice rerun is still no-go.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Video rerun evidence must explicitly say whether failure matched the repaired voice divergent layer or diverged later.

scenarios.scenario-1: The rerun packet set stores deploy, readiness, voice, video, and updated first-divergent-block evidence separately from Phase-19.
scenarios.scenario-2: A blended summary that erases the pre-fix versus post-fix boundary is rejected as insufficient evidence.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The rerun evidence packet must preserve the distinction between the original no-go packet and the repaired rerun results.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The rerun packet must remain split into deploy, readiness, voice, video, and failure sections rather than only a post-fix prose summary.

scenarios.scenario-1: The updated decision states exactly what changed after the repaired rerun and remains bounded to the tested setup.
scenarios.scenario-2: The rerun decision must not overclaim working calls if the repaired wave only moved the divergent block deeper.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The updated decision must compare against the old Phase-19 no-go packet without silently overwriting it.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The updated decision must state separately whether the ingress defect is fixed even if end-to-end call success is still not proven.

scenarios.scenario-1: The new phase explicitly separates the local-client topology from the older SSH-forwarded Desktop path.
scenarios.scenario-2: The topology profile is rejected if it silently reuses the older operator path without comparison semantics.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The profile must explicitly state that the old SSH-forwarded Desktop path remains historical evidence rather than the new execution target.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The profile must keep the future claim bounded to the tested workstation build, tested server pair, and tested network setup only.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'local n0wss-client|127.0.0.1:1080|without ssh -L|local workstation' docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml docs/knowledge-graph.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: The runbook shape makes Telegram Desktop point at a truly local listener instead of an SSH-forwarded remote one.
scenarios.scenario-2: The runbook is rejected if it leaves the operator topology ambiguous.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The runbook must explicitly forbid keeping the old ssh -L path enabled during the local-client wave.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The runbook must specify how the operator proves the listener is local before Telegram is started.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'local bootstrap|trust anchor|local n0wss-client|cleanup' docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml docs/knowledge-graph.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: The local bootstrap shape defines binary, env, trust material, launch, and cleanup surfaces explicitly.
scenarios.scenario-2: The local bootstrap is rejected if it relies on ad hoc workstation mutation with no bounded launch shape.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The bootstrap must define provenance for the local binary and trust material so the future wave can distinguish workstation drift from runtime behavior.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The bootstrap must define a cleanup shape so local reruns do not reuse stale processes or stale listeners.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'local readiness|UDP ASSOCIATE|local listener|local smoke' docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml docs/knowledge-graph.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: The local-client topology requires proof of local listener, local smoke, and local UDP ASSOCIATE readiness before any call attempt.
scenarios.scenario-2: The wave stops if the local workstation path is not proven ready before operator calls start.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The readiness packet must show a raw local UDP ASSOCIATE proof on the same workstation topology that Telegram will use.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: If local readiness is green, later call failure may not be attributed back to generic listener ingress without new contrary evidence.

scenarios.scenario-1: The local-client voice wave captures signaling and media evidence separately.
scenarios.scenario-2: The local-client voice wave must not inherit evidence from the older SSH-forwarded Desktop packet.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The voice packet must classify signaling green and media green separately for the local topology.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: If local UDP ingress is proven but later datagram markers are missing, the packet must promote the next missing marker to the new first divergent block.

scenarios.scenario-1: The local-client video wave remains packetized separately from the local voice wave.
scenarios.scenario-2: Video cannot silently inherit the local voice result.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The video packet must state whether its first divergent layer matches or differs from the local voice packet.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: UI ringing or answer state alone is not valid media success evidence for the local topology.

scenarios.scenario-1: The local-client packet set preserves readiness, voice, video, and failure sections separately.
scenarios.scenario-2: A blended local summary with no topology comparison is rejected as insufficient evidence.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The evidence set must preserve both the older SSH-forwarded packet and the new local-client packet without collapsing them into one historical thread.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The evidence set must keep room for a later external-network hypothesis only after local and governed path markers are classified.

scenarios.scenario-1: The comparison packet explicitly contrasts the old SSH-forwarded path with the new local-client path.
scenarios.scenario-2: The comparison is rejected if it erases the older no-go packet or fails to isolate topology dependence.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The comparison packet must explicitly state whether the new result changes because the topology changed or because the deeper datagram path changed.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Provider or network blocking may be raised only after the comparison packet shows no earlier topology or governed-path divergence.

scenarios.scenario-1: The final decision stays bounded to the tested local-client topology and explicitly compares against the older SSH-forwarded path.
scenarios.scenario-2: The final decision must not overclaim universal Telegram calls success or solve all network blocking regimes.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The final decision must state separately whether the local topology changed the evidence envelope at all.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: If all governed markers are green and calls still fail, only then may the decision classify the remaining risk as likely external network filtering or provider-side blocking.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'hypothesis|Telegram Desktop|external filtering|remote media|SOCKS5 UDP' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: The hypothesis set remains bounded to app behavior, governed datagram path, and external filtering only after earlier layers are classified.
scenarios.scenario-2: The phase must not jump straight to provider or regulator blocking without controlled-probe evidence.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The hypothesis set must preserve the already proven facts from Phase-21 instead of reopening topology questions as unresolved.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The hypothesis set must keep app behavior, datagram-path defects, and external filtering as distinct branches rather than a blended “network problem” bucket.

scenarios.scenario-1: Controlled tooling can open SOCKS5 UDP ASSOCIATE and send bounded datagrams without Telegram UI.
scenarios.scenario-2: The probe path must not blur success of raw association with success of end-to-end datagram exchange.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The tooling must preserve the UDP ASSOCIATE marker and at least one deeper datagram marker in its success path.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The tooling must leave an evidence shape that can distinguish association success, outbound datagram success, and inbound reply success as separate stages.

scenarios.scenario-1: The capture surface is bounded by time and location so app and tunnel traffic can be compared without noisy unbounded dumps.
scenarios.scenario-2: Capture guidance must not rely on unconstrained workstation-wide packet logs that are too noisy to classify.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Capture commands must preserve correlation between workstation events and n0wss runtime markers.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Capture guidance must name the minimal interface, duration, and packet scope needed for later comparison against controlled probes.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'app behavior|bypass|proxy-governed UDP|stalls before media' docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml README.md

scenarios.scenario-1: The classifier distinguishes proxy-governed UDP usage from bypass or pre-media stalling behavior.
scenarios.scenario-2: The classifier must not report a transport-core defect if the app never attempts the governed media path.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Classification must compare workstation capture evidence with runtime markers instead of relying on Telegram UI text alone.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The classifier must explicitly say whether Telegram produced proxy-governed UDP, bypass traffic, or no media-path attempt at all.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'remote media|UDP relay|echo target|inbound|outbound' docs/OPERATORS.md docs/verification-plan.xml docs/development-plan.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: The remote relay path is exercised independently of Telegram and captures outbound and inbound datagram evidence.
scenarios.scenario-2: A remote-media probe must not be mistaken for Telegram app success.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The probe must preserve outbound and inbound relay markers separately so asymmetric failures stay visible.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The probe packet must remain independent from Telegram Desktop symptoms so relay reachability is not inferred from UI behavior.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'blocking boundary|external filtering|app behavior mismatch|datagram-path defect' README.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: The boundary packet distinguishes app behavior, governed-path defects, and only then plausible external filtering.
scenarios.scenario-2: Provider-blocking language must not appear before controlled probes and app classification are complete.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The boundary packet must point to the first still-unresolved layer rather than repeat already-closed topology questions.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: External filtering may be elevated only if both the controlled probe and the remote media probe leave no earlier unresolved governed-path gap.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'next-step|next architecture|workaround phase|bounded decision' README.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: The final decision names the next architectural phase without overstating certainty beyond the evidence packet.
scenarios.scenario-2: The final decision must not claim universal provider blocking or universal Telegram incompatibility without bounded proof.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The decision must say whether the next phase is transport, app-specific handoff, or external-network workaround oriented.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The decision must preserve the bounded reason why that next phase is needed, using the blocking-boundary packet rather than a generic “calls still do not work” summary.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'round-trip|outbound|inbound|echo target|controlled probe' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: The hypothesis set stays bounded to controlled datagram round-trip layers and does not reopen already resolved topology or ingress questions.
scenarios.scenario-2: The plan collapses outbound success, inbound timeout, and Telegram UI symptoms into one blended hypothesis set.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The hypothesis packet must explicitly preserve the already proven facts: UDP ASSOCIATE ingress is alive and the Telegram call packet reached the local SOCKS5 TCP envelope.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The remaining unresolved layers must be named as controlled outbound reachability, remote echo-target ingress, and inbound reply return.

scenarios.scenario-1: The controlled remote echo target has one bounded startup shape, one bounded capture window, and one bounded cleanup shape.
scenarios.scenario-2: The echo target remains ad hoc or stale across probes, making remote ingress evidence ambiguous.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The echo target packet must prove that the target UDP port is listening before the probe starts.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The bounded evidence must make it clear whether a zero-packet capture means no ingress or only a missing capture window.

scenarios.scenario-1: The controlled probe proves association success and remote-target ingress as a bounded outbound trajectory.
scenarios.scenario-2: Probe output says sent, but no downstream datagram marker or no remote-target ingress evidence appears in the same bounded window.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Association success alone is not enough; outbound classification requires a deeper marker or remote-target ingress evidence.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The first missing outbound marker must be named explicitly if the packet stops before the echo target.

scenarios.scenario-1: The controlled probe receives a bounded inbound reply that can be correlated to the same association and echo target.
scenarios.scenario-2: Outbound is proven but inbound reply is missing, times out, or cannot be correlated to the same bounded probe window.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Inbound success requires reply evidence at the probe, not just remote optimism.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: If inbound reply is missing, the packet must say whether remote ingress was observed or remained unproven.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'tcpdump|pcap|correlation|bounded window|local loopback|remote echo' docs/OPERATORS.md docs/verification-plan.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: Probe stdout, local markers, and remote capture all belong to one bounded probe attempt and can be compared without rereading larger dumps.
scenarios.scenario-2: The evidence bundle mixes different windows or different probes, making round-trip classification impossible.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The correlation packet must preserve separate local and remote evidence while naming the exact probe attempt they belong to.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: A missing remote pcap is a distinct evidence gap and must not be silently treated as zero ingress.

scenarios.scenario-1: The first unresolved datagram layer is named precisely without reopening Telegram UI ambiguity or external filtering too early.
scenarios.scenario-2: The decision jumps from inbound timeout to provider blame or back to Telegram UI without resolving the controlled datagram packet.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The blocking boundary must distinguish association success, outbound trace success, remote ingress success, and inbound reply success as separate layers.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The first unresolved layer must be named in datagram terms before any new app-specific phase is justified.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'next phase|code-fix|relay repair|probe correction|app-specific' README.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: The final decision names the next datagram-focused or app-focused phase based on the resolved round-trip boundary.
scenarios.scenario-2: The final decision remains vague or recommends another blind Telegram rerun on the same unresolved datagram boundary.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The decision must say whether the next step is a datagram-path repair, relay repair, probe correction, or only then an app-specific Telegram phase.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The decision must preserve the bounded reason for that next step using the datagram blocking-boundary packet.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'repair scope|remote echo-target ingress|transport-side|Telegram rerun' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: The repair hypothesis keeps the unresolved layer bounded between local UDP ingress and proven remote ingress.
scenarios.scenario-2: The plan reopens Telegram UI or provider-blame questions before the transport-side repair boundary is addressed.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The repair hypothesis must explicitly preserve the proven Phase-23 facts: local UDP ASSOCIATE is green and remote echo-target ingress remains unproven.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The phase must remain transport-side until the controlled datagram packet advances.

scenarios.scenario-1: A bounded marker now exists between local relay acceptance and transport-side datagram emission.
scenarios.scenario-2: The packet can still disappear between coarse ingress and emission anchors with no new evidence.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The repair wave must be able to distinguish accepted relay traffic from manager-side dispatch attempts.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The new marker set must stay bounded and deterministic enough for controlled probe comparison.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: The implementation must leave the existing `BLOCK_HANDLE_UDP_ASSOCIATE`, `BLOCK_OPEN_DATAGRAM_ASSOCIATION`, and `BLOCK_SELECT_DATAGRAM_TRANSPORT` anchors intact while adding the missing dispatch-side promotion surface.

scenarios.scenario-1: Outbound datagrams progress beyond local ingress into the manager-owned dispatch path.
scenarios.scenario-2: Association ownership or local dispatch still stalls before the transport carrier is engaged.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The repair must show that local relay acceptance and manager dispatch are no longer collapsed into the same ambiguous event.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: If dispatch still fails, the first failing manager-side layer must be explicit.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: Module-local tests should cover both successful outbound progression and deterministic dispatch failure without requiring live hosts.

scenarios.scenario-1: The repaired path proves that outbound datagrams are emitted through the WSS carrier instead of stopping at local dispatch.
scenarios.scenario-2: Transport selection succeeds conceptually, but no bounded WSS emission evidence appears.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Client-side emission must be evidenced separately from local dispatch success.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: If emission is still absent, the packet must stop before server-side relay blame begins.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: The repair packet must distinguish selector success from actual WSS datagram send success, because those are separate bounded layers.

module-checks.check-2: rg -n 'BLOCK_RELAY_UDP_OUTBOUND|echo target|remote ingress' src/proxy_bridge/udp_relay.rs src/proxy_bridge/udp_relay.test.rs docs/OPERATORS.md docs/verification-plan.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: The server-side relay produces proven remote echo-target ingress in the bounded probe window.
scenarios.scenario-2: Relay intent exists, but remote ingress remains unproven and the boundary still ends before the echo target.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Server-side outbound relay evidence must be separate from remote ingress evidence.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The repair packet must state whether the relay emitted a send even if the target capture remains empty.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: If remote ingress is still absent, the packet must say whether the missing layer is before relay outbound, at relay outbound, or after relay outbound toward the echo target.

scenarios.scenario-1: The post-repair rerun proves that the deepest proven datagram layer moved beyond the Phase-23 boundary.
scenarios.scenario-2: The rerun reproduces the same boundary with no new deeper marker or remote ingress evidence.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The rerun must say explicitly whether remote ingress or inbound reply is now proven.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: If the boundary does not move, the packet must preserve that no Telegram-specific rerun is justified yet.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: The rerun packet must preserve the exact probe payload label and bounded capture window so it can be compared one-to-one with the Phase-23 packet.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'pre-fix|post-fix|Phase-23|comparison packet|repair evidence' docs/OPERATORS.md docs/verification-plan.xml docs/development-plan.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: The repair wave leaves a bounded pre-fix versus post-fix comparison packet naming the layer that moved.
scenarios.scenario-2: The post-repair summary cannot be compared against Phase-23 and leaves progress ambiguous.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The evidence packet must preserve the exact Phase-23 boundary as the baseline for comparison.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The packet must say whether progress reached remote ingress, inbound reply, or neither.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: The comparison packet must explicitly state whether `outbound_result=sent` gained any deeper supporting evidence beyond the Phase-23 baseline.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'Telegram-specific wave|transport-side|datagram repair|post-repair decision' README.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: The decision says clearly whether the datagram path is repaired enough for a new Telegram-specific wave.
scenarios.scenario-2: The decision reverts to a vague “try Telegram again” without proving the transport-side boundary moved.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The decision must distinguish transport-side repair progress from app-specific justification.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The decision must preserve the bounded reason why a Telegram rerun is or is not justified.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: A Telegram-specific rerun is only allowed if the controlled datagram packet advances beyond the Phase-23 boundary with explicit deeper evidence.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'runtime glue|live caller|Telegram-specific rerun|Phase-24' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The hypothesis keeps the blocker bounded to missing live runtime callers and production carrier wiring instead of reopening already-repaired helper logic.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The wording silently collapses runtime glue, helper repair, Telegram UI symptoms, or provider filtering back into one blended blocker.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The live UDP relay socket is read after `UDP ASSOCIATE`, datagrams are normalized, and the runtime loop reaches the manager-owned handoff surface.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: Cancellation or relay-loop shutdown exits deterministically without leaking a live UDP task that can survive past the control socket lifecycle.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: `BLOCK_HANDLE_UDP_ASSOCIATE` appears but the runtime never emits a deeper local receive or forwarding marker, leaving the packet stuck at ingress.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The runtime loop accepts foreign or malformed datagrams without preserving the governed normalization boundary already defined by earlier phases.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: A production runtime implementation satisfies `WssDatagramPath` and emits governed datagram frames through the real websocket task ownership model.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: Runtime carrier evidence stays separate from selector intent so a chosen transport and a real websocket frame emission can be distinguished.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The code still compiles only because tests or mocks implement `WssDatagramPath` while production runtime remains unwired.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: `BLOCK_EMIT_OUTBOUND_DATAGRAM` appears without a corresponding `BLOCK_SEND_WSS_DATAGRAM`, leaving the runtime carrier unproven.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The bridge preserves association ownership while handing one normalized runtime datagram from local ingress to carrier emission.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The bridge does not widen the datagram path into unplanned transports beyond the bounded WSS-backed carrier.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The runtime loop and repaired manager handoff remain disconnected, so helper-level tests stay green while live packets never advance beyond ingress.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The bridge drops association identity or target metadata between local relay ownership and runtime emission.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The server runtime decodes one real WSS datagram frame and invokes the repaired UDP relay outbound helper without collapsing into stream-only handling.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: Inbound relay validation remains deterministic if the runtime later sees a reply from an unexpected source.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: Client-side runtime emission appears, but the server runtime never produces a datagram-receive or relay-outbound marker.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The server-side runtime path handles datagram frames ambiguously enough that stream semantics and datagram semantics cannot be told apart from evidence.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The rerun proves that the deepest controlled datagram layer advanced beyond the stopped Phase-24 runtime-caller boundary.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The rerun either proves remote echo-target ingress or names the next first missing runtime marker explicitly.
success-scenarios.scenario-3: The rerun may stop at inbound timeout only after client-side emission and server-side relay outbound are both proven in the same bounded packet.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The rerun repeats `association-ok` with only local ingress markers and no stronger runtime evidence than Phase-23 or Phase-24.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The rerun produces a mock-only or helper-only packet instead of a real client and server runtime packet tied to one bounded probe window.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The comparison packet preserves both baselines: the Phase-23 local-ingress tuple and the stopped Phase-24 helper-only tuple with no live runtime callers.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The packet states exactly which runtime layer moved after glue work: local receive, manager handoff, WSS emission, server ingress, remote ingress, or inbound reply.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The post-glue packet cannot be distinguished from the earlier Phase-23 or Phase-24 packets, so progress remains ambiguous.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'Telegram-specific calls wave|runtime glue|bounded decision|phase-25' README.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The decision explicitly separates “runtime glue repaired enough for a Telegram-specific rerun” from “runtime datagram blocker still unresolved”.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The decision falls back to vague language such as “try Telegram again” without naming the deepest proven post-glue layer.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'inbound reply|Phase-25|server relay outbound|inbound_result=timeout' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The hypothesis keeps the next blocker bounded to inbound reply return after already-proven outbound runtime glue.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The wording reopens outbound runtime glue, Telegram UI behavior, or provider filtering as if Phase-25 had not already fixed the earlier boundary.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The hypothesis must preserve the exact Phase-25 tuple: SERVER_DATAGRAM_RECEIVED, BLOCK_RELAY_UDP_OUTBOUND, and inbound_result=timeout.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The hypothesis must forbid a Telegram-specific rerun until one deeper inbound marker is proven in the same bounded packet.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The old pending Phase-24 tail is explicitly marked as superseded so execution truth no longer queues it as live work.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The shared artifacts still leave Phase-24 step 6 through 8 looking pending, so the queue remains ambiguous.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Phase-24 must remain superseded in plan, graph, and verification simultaneously.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The relay keeps enough association and remote-peer state to map one inbound reply back to the correct governed association.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The relay-state surface can reject replies from an unexpected peer or stale association deterministically.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The outbound relay send succeeds but the server runtime cannot tell which association should receive the reply.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: Reply state leaks across associations and an inbound packet can be misattributed to the wrong caller.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Relay state must preserve association identity, reply peer, and return metadata separately from raw socket handles.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Reply mapping must remain bounded to the specific association opened by the same controlled probe window.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The server runtime reads one inbound UDP reply after relay outbound and normalizes it into an association-scoped inbound envelope.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The server receive loop classifies unexpected-source or malformed replies deterministically without silently dropping trajectory evidence.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: Server relay outbound is proven but no inbound receive marker ever appears for the same bounded probe window.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: A reply reaches the server socket, but the runtime cannot distinguish accepted inbound reply from rejected foreign-source traffic.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: One server-side inbound datagram is emitted back over the governed WSS carrier instead of stopping at relay outbound.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The return emission preserves association identity so the client runtime can still map the datagram back to the correct local relay socket.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The server runtime reads an inbound reply but the return datagram never reaches the WSS carrier or client runtime.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The return path emits a frame without preserving association metadata, so the client runtime cannot deliver it deterministically.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The client runtime maps one returned WSS datagram back into the owning local UDP relay socket and the local probe can receive it.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: Client-side inbound delivery remains bounded to the association that opened the original UDP ASSOCIATE path.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The server proves inbound reply handling but the client runtime never emits a local inbound delivery marker or probe-visible reply.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The client receives a returned datagram but cannot map it to the correct relay socket or local address tuple.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The bounded rerun proves `reply-received` or names the next first missing inbound marker after server relay outbound.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The rerun separates `server-received`, `wss-returned`, `client-delivered`, and `reply-received` as distinct evidence layers.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The rerun still ends in `inbound_result=timeout` without any stronger inbound receive or local delivery evidence than Phase-25.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The rerun reports reply success without preserving a bounded correlation to the same probe payload and association window.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The rerun must preserve the Phase-25 baseline if `reply-received` is still absent.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The rerun must name the first missing inbound layer explicitly: server receive, WSS return, client delivery, or probe receive.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The comparison packet preserves the earlier Phase-23, superseded Phase-24, and done Phase-25 baselines while stating exactly which inbound-return layer moved.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The packet keeps reply success or reply timeout separate from server-side or client-side partial progress.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The post-inbound-repair packet cannot be distinguished from the Phase-25 inbound-timeout baseline, so progress remains ambiguous.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The comparison packet must preserve the exact Phase-25 tuple and say whether the new packet moved to server receive, WSS return, client delivery, or full reply receipt.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'reply-received|Telegram-specific wave|inbound reply|Phase-26' README.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The final decision explicitly separates “controlled datagram round-trip complete enough for Telegram-specific verification” from “inbound return still blocked”.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The decision falls back to a vague “try calls again” without proving bounded inbound reply return.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: No Telegram-specific rerun is allowed unless the controlled packet proves inbound reply return or names a deeper first missing inbound marker than Phase-25.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The decision must explicitly state whether the new deepest proven layer is server receive, WSS return, client delivery, or reply receipt.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'Phase-26|server-side inbound receive|return emission|loopback' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The next bounded blocker is explicitly frozen inside server-side inbound receive and return emission after Phase-26.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The hypothesis reopens already-proven outbound glue or Telegram UI diagnosis.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The hypothesis must preserve the completed Phase-26 tuple: proven server-local echo ingress, absent server inbound-return markers, and `inbound_result=timeout`.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The hypothesis must bound the next repair scope to server-side inbound receive and return emit rather than reopening outbound runtime glue or Telegram UI behavior.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The relay session state preserves enough metadata for one inbound reply to map back to the owning association.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: Reply receive cannot map back to the owning relay session deterministically.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The relay-session state must retain enough association metadata to distinguish a valid reply from a stale or foreign-source packet before any return emit is counted as progress.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The live runtime reads one inbound UDP reply after the proven outbound relay step.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The runtime still stops at BLOCK_RELAY_UDP_OUTBOUND and never proves inbound receive.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The first claimed Phase-27 progress must be one bounded server-side inbound receive marker after the already-proven outbound relay step.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: Valid inbound replies are separated cleanly from foreign-source or wrong-association packets.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: Foreign-source or wrong-association packets can be misclassified as progress.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Accepted reply-source evidence must stay separate from return emission or reply success; bounded rejects are valid evidence, not progress.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: A validated inbound reply is emitted back over the governed WSS carrier.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The server runtime receives a reply but still does not emit it back to the client.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Return emit must be classified separately from server receive; `SERVER_DATAGRAM_INBOUND_RECEIVED` alone is not enough to claim deeper progress.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The bounded rerun proves a deeper inbound layer than Phase-26, ideally client delivery or reply-received.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The rerun packet still cannot distinguish server receive, return emit, client delivery, or final reply receipt.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The rerun must classify whether the deepest proven layer is server receive, validated return emit, client local delivery, or full `reply-received`.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: If `reply-received` is still absent, the rerun must preserve the exact Phase-26 baseline while naming the next missing inbound-return layer explicitly.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'Phase-25|Phase-26|server receive|WSS return|client delivery|reply-received' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The comparison packet states exactly which inbound-return layer moved beyond the completed Phase-26 boundary.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The new packet cannot be distinguished from the Phase-26 loopback baseline.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The comparison packet must be anchored explicitly against the completed Phase-25 outbound-glue packet and the completed Phase-26 inbound-timeout packet.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The packet must state whether progress moved to server receive, validated return emit, client delivery, or full reply receipt.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'Telegram-specific wave|server-side inbound|reply-received|Phase-27' README.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The decision explicitly separates “controlled datagram round-trip complete enough for Telegram-specific verification” from “server-side inbound return still blocked”.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The decision falls back to a vague “try calls again” without proving bounded server-side inbound return.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: No Telegram-specific rerun is allowed unless the controlled datagram packet proves a deeper inbound-return layer than the completed Phase-26 boundary.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The decision must explicitly say whether the blocker remains at server inbound receive, validated return emit, client local delivery, or final probe-visible reply.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'Phase-27|round-trip|Telegram calls|transport green|tested Desktop setup' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The profile explicitly freezes generic datagram transport as green baseline and pushes the next Telegram wave into app-facing evidence only.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The profile silently reopens transport repair or widens claims beyond the tested Telegram Desktop setup.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The profile must explicitly anchor the new calls wave against the completed Phase-27 `reply-received` packet rather than against older no-go waves alone.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'reply-received|readiness|same deployment window|SOCKS5 bind|phase27-probe' docs/OPERATORS.md docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The readiness packet proves active services, local SOCKS5 bind, and one bounded `phase27-probe` success in the same deployment window before the first call attempt.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The calls rerun can start without a same-window green controlled probe, making app failure indistinguishable from deploy drift.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The readiness packet must keep deploy provenance, service readiness, local bind proof, and green controlled probe evidence in one bounded same-window envelope.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The voice rerun packet keeps signaling evidence separate from governed media-path evidence and names the first divergent app-facing layer if media still fails.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: Ringing, answered UI, or generic key-exchange symptoms are treated as sufficient proof of media success.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The voice packet must explicitly say whether the observed result is governed media, direct-media behavior, or signaling-only stall.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'video-call|separate from voice|post-roundtrip|Telegram Desktop' docs/verification-plan.xml docs/development-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The video rerun packet remains separate from voice and states whether video matches or diverges from the voice outcome.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The video result silently inherits the voice result or is omitted from the packet model.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The video packet must compare itself against the voice rerun explicitly instead of assuming parity.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The classifier maps the post-roundtrip calls outcome into one bounded app-facing bucket without reopening generic transport repair.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: If voice or video reaches answer or key-exchange state but no green media packet follows, the classifier may choose `signaling-only stall` as long as it says why `direct-media behavior` is still unproven.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The classifier falls back to vague wording such as “calls still broken” without naming the app-facing class.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The classifier must choose among governed media success, direct-media behavior, or signaling-only stall and justify the choice from the new packets.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The classifier must keep key-exchange progress and absent green media in the same bounded packet instead of treating either one as proof of direct-media bypass.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'reconnect|second call attempt|fresh second call|separate' docs/verification-plan.xml docs/development-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The reconnect packet remains separate from the first call and classifies whether the second attempt rebuilds the media path deterministically.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: If the second fresh call attempt repeats the same key-exchange stall as the first attempt, the reconnect packet still counts as valid evidence as long as it is recorded separately.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The reconnect result is omitted or collapsed into the first call result.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The reconnect packet must be anchored as one fresh second call attempt after the first packet finishes, not as a blended continuation.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The reconnect packet must explicitly say whether the second call changed behavior or repeated the same key-exchange symptom.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The evidence packet explicitly compares the new post-roundtrip calls packets against the older no-go calls waves from before Phase-27.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The comparison packet preserves the same-window green readiness probe so the new calls packet cannot be mistaken for the older transport-uncertain waves.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The new packet cannot be distinguished from older calls no-go packets or from the transport-only Phase-27 packet.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The evidence packet must preserve separate readiness, voice, video, reconnect, and classifier sections before the final decision is formed.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The comparison packet must explicitly say that the older no-go waves predated the green Phase-27 round-trip baseline while the new packet does not.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'partially green|still blocked|tested Desktop setup|Telegram calls decision' README.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The final decision states whether the tested Telegram Desktop setup is green, partially green, or still blocked, and it may split voice, video, and reconnect outcomes.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: If the same live window preserved a green controlled datagram probe but all call packets still stop before green media, the final decision may stay fully blocked as long as it names the blocker app-facing rather than transport-facing.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The final decision overclaims universal calls success or reopens generic datagram transport diagnosis despite the completed Phase-27 baseline.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The final decision must stay bounded to the tested Telegram Desktop setup and explicitly say whether any remaining blocker is app-facing rather than transport-facing.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The final decision must say whether voice, video, and reconnect converged on the same no-go class or diverged after the green round-trip baseline.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'post-Phase-28|app-facing|green transport|tested Desktop setup' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The hypothesis packet freezes generic transport as green and moves the next wave fully into Telegram-specific media behavior.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The hypothesis packet explicitly says that Phase-28 already ended as app-facing no-go for voice, video, and reconnect on the tested Desktop setup.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The new phase silently reopens generic datagram repair or widens claims beyond the tested Desktop setup.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The hypothesis packet must preserve both sides of the boundary at once: green controlled datagram baseline and app-facing calls no-go.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'Desktop handoff|proxy handoff|same-window|phase27-probe|bounded call action' docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The handoff profile preserves one exact Desktop proxy path and keeps same-window readiness attached to later media packets.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The handoff packet makes it possible to distinguish proxy-setup failure from later Telegram-specific media behavior without rereading older calls waves.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The operator path stays ambiguous enough that a later media packet could collapse into another setup issue.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The handoff packet must preserve the exact Desktop proxy shape, local forward state when applicable, and the same-window green `phase27-probe` precondition.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The capture packet keeps workstation, WSS, and app-facing evidence bounded enough to compare later media behavior deterministically.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The capture packet can distinguish answer or key-exchange progress from absent green media without depending on vague UI prose alone.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The capture guidance is too weak to separate signaling progress from missing green media.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The capture packet must keep workstation-facing evidence separate from preserved WSS or transport-green baseline evidence.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The capture packet must name the loopback, uplink, and remote server capture surfaces explicitly so later agents can compare them instead of blending them.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'voice media|signaling-only stall|governed-media progress|app-facing' docs/verification-plan.xml docs/development-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The voice classifier distinguishes post-answer signaling progress from actual green media behavior on the tested Desktop setup.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: If answer or key-exchange emoji appear but no green media packet follows, the voice classifier may stay in `signaling-only stall` as long as it says why that does not prove governed media success.
success-scenarios.scenario-3: If the operator reports a repeated key-exchange stall and the bounded capture packet completed, the classifier may still stay `signaling-only stall` as long as it keeps the capture packet attached.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The voice packet is treated as generic failure text without a bounded media classification.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The voice classifier must explicitly choose among signaling-only stall, governed-media progress, or another bounded app-facing media behavior.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The voice classifier must preserve the bounded capture packet when the app symptom repeats an earlier key-exchange stall.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'video media|separate from voice|diverges earlier|app-facing' docs/verification-plan.xml docs/development-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The video classifier stays separate from voice and states whether video matches or diverges from the voice media layer.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: If video stalls earlier than voice, that earlier boundary is preserved explicitly instead of being merged into the voice classifier.
success-scenarios.scenario-3: If the bounded video packet repeats the same key-exchange stall as voice, the classifier may still choose the same no-go class as long as the video packet keeps its own capture evidence.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The video packet silently inherits the voice classifier.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The video classifier must explicitly compare itself against the voice classifier instead of assuming parity.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The video classifier must preserve its own bounded capture packet even when it converges on the same no-go class as voice.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'proxy behavior|green baseline|older calls packets|Telegram-specific' README.md docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The comparison packet explains the new Telegram-specific media boundary without reopening generic transport diagnosis.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The comparison packet states whether voice and video converge on the same app-facing class or diverge by media profile.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The comparison packet collapses back into generic proxy or transport uncertainty.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The comparison packet must preserve the completed Phase-28 no-go packet and the same-window green transport baseline at the same time.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: If voice and video converge on the same no-go class, the comparison packet must still say that each packet carried its own bounded capture evidence.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'handoff|capture|voice|video|comparison|Telegram media evidence' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The evidence packet keeps handoff, capture, voice, video, and comparison sections separate before the final decision.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The evidence packet remains distinguishable from the completed Phase-28 calls packet even if the underlying app-facing no-go class stays unchanged.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The new media packet cannot be distinguished from the completed Phase-28 calls packet.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The evidence packet must preserve separate handoff, capture, voice, video, and comparison sections before the final decision is formed.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The evidence packet must explicitly compare the completed Phase-29 media packet against the completed Phase-28 calls packet.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'Telegram media decision|tested Desktop setup|workaround phase|alternate app topology|no-go' README.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The final decision stays bounded to the tested Desktop setup and says whether a workaround phase, alternate topology, or continued no-go is justified.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The final decision may keep the setup fully no-go as long as it explicitly says that the transport baseline stayed green and the remaining blocker stayed Telegram-specific.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The final decision widens claims or reopens generic transport diagnosis instead of staying Telegram-specific.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The final decision must remain bounded to the tested Desktop setup and explicitly say whether the next justified phase is a workaround phase, alternate topology, or continued no-go.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The final decision must preserve the green transport baseline while explaining why the completed media packet still remains no-go.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'workaround-only|alternate app topology|Phase-29|green transport baseline' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml
module-checks.check-2: rg -n 'tested Telegram Desktop setup|no-go class|Telegram-specific workaround' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The hypothesis packet freezes future work inside Telegram-specific workaround space without reopening generic transport diagnosis.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The hypothesis packet keeps the completed Phase-29 no-go media packet alive as the only historical baseline for workaround comparison.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The new workaround phase silently reuses Phase-29 as if it were a generic transport rerun.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The hypothesis widens claims beyond the tested Telegram Desktop setup before any alternate-topology packet exists.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The hypothesis wording must explicitly preserve the green transport baseline and forbid reopening generic datagram repair in Phase-30.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The hypothesis wording must say that the next question is whether topology changes the Telegram no-go class, not whether transport is still green.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'alternate topology|bounded alternate|Phase-29 Desktop handoff|phase27-probe' docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml
module-checks.check-2: rg -n 'what changed|what stayed fixed|same-window green' docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The alternate topology packet stays explicitly separate from the exact Phase-29 Desktop handoff route.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The packet names exactly which handoff element changed and which transport preconditions remain unchanged.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The workaround wave silently reuses the exact Phase-29 Desktop route and therefore cannot classify topology impact.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The alternate-topology packet changes more than one uncontrolled variable and destroys comparison value.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The handoff profile must preserve the same green `phase27-probe` precondition while changing one bounded app-topology dimension.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The workaround capture packet stays comparable with the completed Phase-29 media packet.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The capture packet makes it possible to separate alternate-topology handoff proof from voice and video outcomes.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The workaround capture packet cannot be compared directly against Phase-29.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The capture packet collapses handoff proof and media outcome into one blended transcript.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'alternate voice|voice attempt|same no-go class|Phase-29' docs/verification-plan.xml docs/development-plan.xml
module-checks.check-2: rg -n 'separate voice packet|changed the no-go class|reproduced it' docs/verification-plan.xml docs/development-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The alternate-topology voice packet states whether the no-go class changed relative to Phase-29.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The voice packet stays bounded to one alternate-topology attempt and one media classification outcome.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The alternate voice packet is missing or collapses into the old Phase-29 voice packet.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The voice packet reopens transport diagnosis instead of staying app-facing above the green baseline.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'alternate video|video attempt|separate from voice|Phase-29' docs/verification-plan.xml docs/development-plan.xml
module-checks.check-2: rg -n 'video packet|no-go class changed|separate from alternate voice' docs/verification-plan.xml docs/development-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The alternate-topology video packet stays separate from the alternate voice packet and states whether the no-go class changed.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The video packet proves whether video diverges earlier, later, or at the same layer as alternate voice.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The alternate video packet silently inherits the alternate voice result.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The video packet cannot be compared against the completed Phase-29 video packet.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'workaround comparison|alternate topology|Phase-29|same no-go class' README.md docs/verification-plan.xml
module-checks.check-2: rg -n 'changed the no-go class|reproduced the no-go class|voice|video' README.md docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The comparison packet explicitly says whether alternate topology changed the Telegram no-go class or preserved it.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The comparison packet keeps separate voice and video deltas against the completed Phase-29 media packet.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The comparison packet cannot distinguish Phase-30 from the completed Phase-29 packet.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The comparison packet silently treats one improved media profile as universal success for the tested setup.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The comparison packet must state whether the alternate topology changed the no-go class for voice, video, both, or neither.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'workaround evidence|alternate topology|voice|video|comparison' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The workaround evidence packet keeps alternate handoff, capture, voice, video, and comparison sections separate.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The evidence packet stays directly comparable with the completed Phase-29 packet rather than replacing it.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The workaround packet cannot be distinguished from the completed Phase-29 media packet.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The evidence packet omits either the alternate-topology handoff packet or the comparison packet and therefore cannot support a bounded decision.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'workaround decision|alternate topology helps|still no-go|another Telegram-specific branch' README.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml
module-checks.check-2: rg -n 'tested Telegram setup|bounded decision|Phase-29|alternate topology only' README.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The final decision says whether alternate topology helps, still stays no-go, or points to another Telegram-specific branch.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The final decision explicitly limits any improvement claim to the tested Telegram setup and the exact alternate topology.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The final decision widens claims beyond the tested Telegram setup or reopens generic transport diagnosis.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The final decision fails to say whether the alternate topology changed the no-go class relative to Phase-29.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The final decision must choose one bounded outcome: alternate topology helps, alternate topology still no-go, or another Telegram-specific branch is needed.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'deeper workaround|Phase-30|green transport baseline|Telegram-specific' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The hypothesis packet freezes future work inside Telegram-specific app-variant space without reopening topology or transport diagnosis.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The hypothesis packet keeps the completed Phase-29 and Phase-30 no-go classes visible as the historical app-facing baseline.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The new phase silently reopens Phase-29 or Phase-30 as if they were incomplete transport or topology reruns.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The hypothesis packet fails to state that future work is limited to one bounded Telegram-specific variant per wave.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The deeper-workaround boundary must remain strictly above the preserved green `phase27-probe` transport baseline.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The app-variant packet changes exactly one Telegram-specific dimension while preserving the same green transport baseline.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The handoff packet says explicitly what changed relative to the completed Phase-30 topology and what stayed fixed from the preserved transport baseline.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The variant packet changes multiple uncontrolled app variables and destroys comparison value.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The app-variant packet silently reuses the completed Phase-30 route without naming a new Telegram-specific dimension.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The handoff packet must preserve the same-window green `phase27-probe` precondition while changing only one bounded Telegram-specific app variable.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The deeper-workaround capture packet stays directly comparable with the completed Phase-29 and Phase-30 packets.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: The capture packet keeps handoff evidence separate from the later voice, video, and comparison packets.

failure-scenarios.scenario-1: The new capture packet cannot be compared directly against the earlier Telegram media packets.
failure-scenarios.scenario-2: The capture packet collapses the app-variant handoff evidence into the later media outcome packet.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The capture surface must stay reusable for separate voice and video packets under the same app-variant contract.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'variant voice|voice attempt|no-go class|Phase-29|Phase-30' docs/verification-plan.xml docs/development-plan.xml

success-scenarios.scenario-1: The app-variant voice packet states whether the no-go class changed relative to the completed Phase-29 and Phase-30 voice packets.
